title: Aloo Paratha
description: Aloo Paratha is a classic North Indian flatbread stuffed with a flavorful spiced potato filling. Crispy on the outside and soft inside, it is traditionally served with yogurt, pickle, butter, or chai, making it a popular breakfast and comfort meal.

ingredients.title: For the Filling
items: ¼ tsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare the Dough
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine wheat flour and salt.
Gradually add water and knead into a soft, pliable dough.
Cover with a damp cloth and rest for 20–30 minutes.

instruction.title: Step 2: Make the Filling
steps: Mash the boiled potatoes until smooth.
Add green chilies, ginger, coriander, cumin seeds, garam masala, chili powder, turmeric, salt, and onion (if using).
Mix well to form a uniform filling.

instruction.title: Step 3: Shape the Parathas
steps: Divide dough into equal-sized balls (about golf ball size).
Roll each ball into a small disc.
Place 1–2 tbsp of potato filling in the center.
Bring the edges together to seal the filling completely.
Gently flatten and roll into a 6–7 inch round paratha, being careful not to let the filling spill out.

instruction.title: Step 4: Cook the Parathas
steps: Heat a tawa or skillet over medium-high heat.
Place the rolled paratha on the hot tawa.
Cook for 1–2 minutes until bubbles appear, then flip.
Apply ghee or oil on the cooked side and flip again.
Cook until both sides are golden brown and slightly crisp.

instruction.title: Step 5: Serve Hot
steps: Serve immediately with yogurt, pickle, or a dollop of butter.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Serve with yogurt, pickle, or a dollop of butter.
Pair with masala chai for a traditional North Indian breakfast.

chefTips: Use slightly dry mashed potatoes; too much moisture makes rolling difficult.
Resting the dough ensures softer parathas.
Adjust spices in the filling according to your taste preference.
For a restaurant-style finish, cook on high heat for a short time to get slight crispness.
